content: Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD) develops following exposure to actual/threatened death, serious injury, or sexual violence through direct experience, witnessing, learning about a close other's trauma, or repeated extreme exposure to aversive details (e.g., first responders). Core symptom clusters: intrusion (recollections, nightmares, flashbacks, distress to cues), avoidance (trauma-related thoughts/feelings/external reminders), negative alterations in cognition/mood (amnesia, negative beliefs, blame, negative affect, detachment, anhedonia), and arousal/reactivity (irritability, recklessness, hypervigilance, startle, concentration problems, sleep disturbance). Symptoms persist >1 month causing impairment. Lifetime prevalence ~6-9%, higher in women.
